mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trend in FEDFUNDS over the prior 6 months reflects an active Fed tightening cycle that raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, compressing valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 3.26% up from 1.00% in mid-2004, indicating ongoing tightening
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and 20d forward returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or returns during rising-rate periods are not significantly lower than the full-sample mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE falls below 5.0% and stays there for two consecutive months, labor market tightness raises wage-cost concerns for equity investors, triggering short-horizon risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.0% as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint occurrence of a rising FEDFUNDS trend alongside decelerating INDPRO growth signals that monetary tightening is beginning to bite into real output, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as investors price a slowdown-plus-tightening regime that neither series predicts alone.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rising 3-month trend AND INDPRO 3-month annualized growth below its 12-month average
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with growth deceleration
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) relative to the additive single-series model, or 60d forward returns under joint trigger are not significantly more negative than under either single-series trigger alone.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a month-over-month decline in HOUST signals that rising rates are suppressing rate-sensitive housing demand, foreshadowing broader consumer demand weakness and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days; the same HOUST decline in an easing or neutral rate regime carries no such signal.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines month-over-month while FEDFUNDS is above its 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 2004k units amid FEDFUNDS at 3.26%
failure_condition: HOUST declines within tightening regimes are not followed by below-median 20d SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or the regime-conditional effect disappears when tested against an easing-regime control sample.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent positive 3-month trend in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial output and corporate earnings capacity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon as investors extrapolate continued production growth into forward earnings estimates.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO 3-month trend and 60d forward returns is below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or returns are not significantly higher in positive-INDPRO-trend periods than negative-trend periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month acceleration in RSAFS above its trailing 6-month average signals robust consumer spending momentum, which drives short-horizon positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as retail-sensitive earnings expectations are revised upward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth exceeds its trailing 6-month average growth rate
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns following RSAFS acceleration signals are not significantly positive (p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter acceleration in GDPC1 growth signals strengthening real economic momentum that supports higher forward corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon, though the signal is attenuated by high revision risk inherent to preliminary GDP releases.
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 QoQ growth acceleration and 60d forward returns falls below 0.03 across two consecutive GDP vintages, or the sign of the relationship flips after data revision.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation crosses above 3.0%, investors price in an elevated risk of further Fed tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation concern
failure_condition: 20d forward returns following CPI-threshold triggers are not significantly negative (p > 0.05), or the effect reverses when FEDFUNDS is simultaneously falling.
